stem: A circle in the xy-plane has its center at (3, −2). Line k is tangent to this circle at the point (5, 5). What is the slope of line k?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. The slope of the radius from (3, −2) to (5, 5) is (5 − (−2))/(5 − 3) = 7/2. The tangent at a point is perpendicular to the radius, so its slope is the negative reciprocal: −2/7.

Choice A gives the radius's slope. Choices B and C have wrong signs or reciprocals.
